methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Christiaan Jordaan and Andrew Minett. The company has raised $22M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside a broader climate tech universe of 8 private firms and 1 public comparable.

The peer-median method, the primary triangulation approach applicable to Sicona's stage and sector, yields a valuation of $175M. This median is derived from four direct comparables valued between $110M (Steady Energy) and $1.8B (Watershed), with an interquartile range suggesting tighter clustering in the $150M–$200M band. The outlier valuation (Watershed at $1.8B) reflects heterogeneity within Series A climate tech, likely driven by variable technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market timing.

The triangulated valuation range of $149M to $201M, centered at $175M, reflects the median peer approach. Key uncertainties include limited methodological triangulation (single method applied), the wide dispersion among the peer set itself, and Sicona's relative position within that distribution on dimensions of technology differentiation, capital intensity, and pathway to commercialization. Refinement would benefit from revenue multiples or cost-per-unit analysis if available, and clearer delineation of whether Sicona's battery chemistry and production readiness map to the higher or lower quartile of the peer set.
